---
pagetitle: "Ran Wang | Academic Website"
page-layout: full
toc: false
---

<a class="lang-switch hero-lang" href="/zh/">中文</a>

<div class="hero-name" role="heading" aria-level="1">Ran Wang</div>

<div class="hero-kicker">Urban &amp; Regional Economics · Scientific Mobility · Regional Innovation</div>

<div class="hero-summary">I am an empirical researcher trained in economic geography. I study scientific mobility, institutional platforms, and regional innovation using longitudinal data on scientists, institutions, publications, and cities. My work combines discrete-choice models, network analysis, and spatial data methods.</div>

<div class="hero-actions">
<a class="btn btn-hero-primary" href="research.html">View Research</a>
<a class="btn btn-hero-secondary" href="assets/cv/Ran_Wang_CV_Public.pdf">Download CV</a>
<a class="btn btn-hero-outline" href="projects.html">Selected Projects</a>
<a class="btn btn-hero-outline" href="https://github.com/Olivia-wr-research">GitHub</a>
</div>

<div class="photo-credit">Photo: John Rodenn Castillo / Unsplash</div>

<section class="panel panel-light">
<h2>Research Focus</h2>
<p>My research examines scientific mobility, institutional platforms, and regional innovation through longitudinal data on scientists, institutions, publications, and cities. I focus on clear definitions of affiliation, mobility, collaboration, and place, and on empirical designs that connect individual trajectories with urban and regional research systems.</p>
</section>

<section class="panel panel-muted">
<h2>Research Themes</h2>
<p><strong>Scientific Mobility and Destination Choice.</strong> I study how scientists move across institutions and cities, with attention to information available before a move. Current work compares observed destinations with event-specific candidate cities.</p>
<p><strong>Regional Innovation and Urban Networks.</strong> This work examines how cities and regions differ in research capacity, institutional platforms, and positions within mobility and collaboration networks. The emphasis is on observed associations rather than causal claims beyond the research design.</p>
<p><strong>Relational Data and Network Methods.</strong> I build scientist-institution-city data that keep affiliations, publications, collaboration ties, and mobility events analytically distinct. This supports network analysis, spatial comparison, and reproducible workflow checks.</p>
</section>

<section class="panel panel-dark">
<h2>Selected Work</h2>
<p><strong>Accepted Article.</strong> <em>Spatial Structure and Determinants of Top Global Biomedical Scientists' Mobility from an Urban Network Perspective</em>.</p>
<p><strong>Working Paper.</strong> <em>Pre-move Publication-Location Exposure and Destination Choice among Elite Biomedical Scientists</em>.</p>
<p><strong>Public Repository.</strong> <em>Author-Institution Linkage and Geocoding Feasibility Audit</em>. <a class="text-link" href="https://github.com/Olivia-wr-research/author-institution-city-linkage">View repository</a></p>
</section>

<section class="panel panel-light">
<h2>Quick Links</h2>
<p>
<a class="btn btn-primary" href="research.html">Research</a>
<a class="btn btn-primary" href="projects.html">Projects</a>
<a class="btn btn-primary" href="cv.html">CV</a>
<a class="btn btn-primary" href="contact.html">Contact</a>
</p>
</section>
